name: Community Bank Of Pickens County
description_short: Community Bank of Pickens County is a locally owned and operated bank established in 2000, serving its community with competitive deposit and loan products across two locations.
description_long: Community Bank of Pickens County was founded in 2000 with a second branch location added in 2008. The bank is locally owned and locally operated, emphasizing community ties and local decision-making. With over 60 employees, the institution positions itself as a relationship-driven alternative to larger national banks, focusing on serving the Pickens County area with personalized banking services. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking services. For personal customers, they provide online banking with 24/7 account access, mobile banking via iOS and Android apps, bill pay, fund transfers, check and deposit imaging, loan payments, and customized account alerts. Business customers have access to multiple account management, employee login credentials, ACH file transfers, direct deposit payroll, merchant capture capabilities, and merchant services for credit card processing. Additional services include mobile wallet integration (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay), touchtone telephone banking, and ATM access. Community Bank of Pickens County distinguishes itself through its emphasis on local ownership and decision-making, positioning relationship-based banking against larger institutions. The bank offers competitive products in both deposit and loan categories, and maintains modern digital banking infrastructure including mobile apps and online platforms. Their merchant services and business banking features suggest they actively serve small business customers beyond basic retail banking. The bank provides solid standard banking services with modern digital capabilities, making it suitable for personal and small business banking needs in the Pickens County area. However, the website provides limited information about specific loan products, interest rates, fees, or deposit account terms that would allow prospective customers to assess competitiveness. The bank's local focus means services are likely limited to its geographic footprint, and there is no mention of specialized products like investment services, wealth management, or comprehensive business lending solutions.
services: Personal online banking with 24/7 account access
Account fund transfers between personal accounts
Online bill pay and scheduled payments
Business online banking with multiple account access
ACH file transfers for business customers
Mobile banking app (iOS and Android) with mobile check deposit
Mobile wallet integration (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
Merchant capture and credit card processing services
pros: Locally owned and operated with local decision-making authority
Mobile banking app with InstaBalance feature and mobile wallet support (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay)
24/7 online banking and bill pay services for personal customers
Business banking with multiple account access, ACH file transfers, and direct deposit payroll
Mobile check deposit capabilities for both personal and business customers
Merchant capture and merchant services for business customers
Free mobile banking app optimized for iOS and Android devices
cons: Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, or fee schedules for comparison
Limited geographic footprint restricted to Pickens County area with only two branch locations
No information provided about loan products, terms, or lending criteria
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced business lending solutions
Limited transparency about FDIC insurance coverage and deposit account tiers
best_for: Pickens County residents seeking community-focused personal banking with modern digital tools
Small business owners in the local area needing merchant services and business banking solutions
Customers prioritizing local ownership and relationship-based banking over national institutions
diagnosis: Community Bank of Pickens County is best suited for personal and small business customers in the Pickens County area who value local ownership and relationship banking. The main caveat is that the bank's geographic limitation to two local branches and lack of disclosed product details (rates, fees, loan terms) make it difficult for prospective customers to evaluate competitiveness without direct contact.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Community Bank of Pickens County is a locally owned bank established in 2000 serving its community with deposit and loan products.
